records.ety: [OE. drane a dronebee, AS. drān; akin to OS. drān, OHG. treno, G. drohne, Dan. drone, cf. Gr. <?/ a kind of wasp, dial. Gr. <?/ drone. Prob. named fr. the droning sound. See Drone, v. i.]

defs: The male of bees, esp. of the honeybee. It gathers no honey. See Honeybee.
quotes: All with united force combine to drive The lazy drones from the laborious hive.

defs: One who lives on the labors of others; a lazy, idle fellow; a sluggard.
quotes: By living as a drone,to be an unprofitable and unworthy member of so noble and learned a society.

defs: That which gives out a grave or monotonous tone or dull sound; as: (a) A drum. [Obs.] Halliwell. (b) The part of the bagpipe containing the two lowest tubes, which always sound the key note and the fifth.
A humming or deep murmuring sound.
quotes: The monotonous drone of the wheel.

defs: A monotonous bass, as in a pastoral composition.

records.ety: [Cf. (for sense 1) D. dreunen, G. dröhnen, Icel. drynja to roar, drynr a roaring, Sw. dröna to bellow, drone, Dan. dröne, Goth. drunjus sound, Gr. <?/ dirge, <?/ to cry aloud, Skr. dhran to sound. Cf. Drone, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Droned (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Droning.]

defs: To utter or make a low, dull, monotonous, humming or murmuring sound.
quotes: Where the beetle wheels his droning flight.

defs: To love in idleness; to do nothing.
